proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e == 0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.
Cell lines with `kras_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation == 0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e + C(lineage) + C(screen_batch) + C(culture_type) + C(media_serum_pct) + cas9_activity_score + growth_rate_doublings_per_day
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted `braf_v600e` coefficient was -0.5545 dependency-score units, indicating markedly stronger BRAF dependency in BRAF V600E cell lines.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_EGFR ~ egfr_amplification + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted `egfr_amplification` coefficient was -0.3489, supporting EGFR amplification as the dominant EGFR-dependency feature.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_ERBB2 ~ erbb2_amplification + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted `erbb2_amplification` coefficient was -0.4325, supporting strong ERBB2 dependency in ERBB2-amplified lines.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted `kras_mutation` coefficient was -0.3095, supporting KRAS-mutant selective KRAS dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among cell lines with `braf_v600e == 1`, `lineage == skin` cell lines have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than non-skin BRAF V600E cell lines.
Among cell lines with `kras_mutation == 1`, `lineage == pancreatic` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than non-pancreatic KRAS-mutant cell lines.
Cell lines with both `egfr_amplification == 1` and `lineage == brain` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines with both `erbb2_amplification == 1` and `lineage == breast` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch test within braf_v600e == 1; also OLS interaction dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e * I(lineage == 'skin') + screen covariates
analyses.result_summary: Within BRAF V600E lines, skin lines were only -0.0103 units more negative than non-skin lines (p=0.295), and the BRAF V600E by skin interaction was not significant (interaction -0.0128, p=0.282).

analyses.code: Welch test within kras_mutation == 1; also OLS interaction dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation * I(lineage == 'pancreatic') + screen covariates
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS-mutant lines, pancreatic lines were not more KRAS dependent than non-pancreatic KRAS-mutant lines (effect +0.0004, p=0.955); the interaction was also null (-0.0025, p=0.818).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_EGFR ~ I(egfr_amplification == 1 and lineage == 'brain') + screen/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: The EGFR-amplified brain subgroup (n=815) had more negative `dependency_EGFR` than all other lines; adjusted subgroup coefficient was -0.3370.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_ERBB2 ~ I(erbb2_amplification == 1 and lineage == 'breast') + screen/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: The ERBB2-amplified breast subgroup (n=1121) had more negative `dependency_ERBB2` than all other lines; adjusted subgroup coefficient was -0.4158.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss == 0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.
The subgroup with both `nf1_loss == 1` and `lineage == brain` has especially negative `dependency_RIT1` scores, and `lineage == brain` enhances the NF1-loss effect on RIT1 dependency.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted `nf1_loss` coefficient was -0.0955, supporting stronger RIT1 dependency in NF1-loss lines.

analyses.code: OLS subgroup indicator and interaction: dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss * I(lineage == 'brain') + screen covariates
analyses.result_summary: The NF1-loss brain subgroup (n=329) had adjusted effect -0.1384 vs all other lines (p=9.58e-13). The NF1-loss by brain interaction was -0.0579 (p=0.00582), indicating brain lineage enhances the NF1-loss RIT1 dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `apc_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation == 0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.
Higher `wnt_activity_score` is associated with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.

Cell lines with `lineage == colorectal`, `apc_mutation == 1`, and high `wnt_activity_score` (top quartile, `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`) have much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_KIF18A ~ apc_mutation + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted `apc_mutation` coefficient was -0.1469, supporting an APC-associated KIF18A dependency signal.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_KIF18A ~ z(wnt_activity_score) + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: Each 1-SD increase in `wnt_activity_score` was associated with a -0.0440 change in `dependency_KIF18A`.

analyses.code: OLS subgroup indicator: dependency_KIF18A ~ I(lineage == 'colorectal' and apc_mutation == 1 and wnt_activity_score >= Q75) + screen/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT subgroup (n=3133) was very strongly KIF18A dependent, with adjusted subgroup coefficient -0.8596.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the `lineage == colorectal`, `apc_mutation == 1`, high-`wnt_activity_score` subgroup, `smad4_loss == 1` suppresses KIF18A dependency, so the complete strongest subgroup is `lineage == colorectal`, `apc_mutation == 1`, high `wnt_activity_score`, and `smad4_loss == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch test within colorectal/APC/high-WNT subgroup comparing smad4_loss == 1 vs smad4_loss == 0
analyses.result_summary: Within colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT lines, `smad4_loss == 1` shifted `dependency_KIF18A` upward by +1.0433 (less dependent): mean -0.2557 with SMAD4 loss vs -1.2990 with SMAD4 intact.

analyses.code: Welch test for complete subgroup I(lineage == 'colorectal' and apc_mutation == 1 and wnt_activity_score >= Q75 and smad4_loss == 0) vs all other lines
analyses.result_summary: The complete SMAD4-intact colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT subgroup (n=2571) had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 vs -0.2530 in all other lines, effect -1.0460.

analyses.code: OLS interaction: dependency_KIF18A ~ I(colorectal/APC/high-WNT) * smad4_loss + screen covariates
analyses.result_summary: The interaction between the colorectal/APC/high-WNT subgroup and `smad4_loss` was +1.0393 (p underflowed to 0), confirming that SMAD4 loss suppresses the dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `myc_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `myc_amplification == 0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.

Cell lines with both `myc_amplification == 1` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (top quartile, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other cell lines.
Higher `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores, and the high-MYC/high-growth subgroup has more negative `dependency_DHX9` than other lines.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_RPL11 ~ myc_amplification + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted `myc_amplification` coefficient was -0.1043, supporting stronger RPL11 dependency in MYC-amplified lines.

analyses.code: Welch and adjusted subgroup test for I(myc_amplification == 1 and growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= Q75)
analyses.result_summary: The MYC-amplified/high-growth subgroup (n=2557) had unadjusted effect -0.1303 (p=1.10e-68) and adjusted subgroup coefficient -0.1084 (p=1.04e-46). The formal MYC-amplification by high-growth interaction was not significant (interaction -0.0151, p=0.105).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_DHX9 ~ z(rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm) + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9/growth covariates; subgroup high growth and high MYC RNA
analyses.result_summary: Each 1-SD increase in MYC RNA was associated with -0.0167 lower `dependency_DHX9` (p=1.45e-26). The high-growth/high-MYC subgroup had adjusted effect -0.0338 (p=4.24e-07), but the interaction was not significant at 0.05 (p=0.0878).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (top quartile, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than lower-growth cell lines, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates other than growth itself.

Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (top quartile, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than lower-growth cell lines, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates other than growth itself.
The subgroup with `lineage == prostate` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` has more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than other cell lines, beyond the broad growth association.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_POLR2A ~ I(growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= Q75) + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9 covariates
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had adjusted `dependency_POLR2A` coefficient -0.1044, supporting a strong growth-linked POLR2A dependency.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_TMED10 ~ I(growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= Q75) + C(lineage) + screen/culture/media/cas9 covariates
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had adjusted `dependency_TMED10` coefficient -0.0370, a statistically strong but smaller growth-linked effect.

analyses.code: OLS subgroup and interaction tests for I(lineage == 'prostate') * high_growth
analyses.result_summary: The prostate/high-growth subgroup had adjusted coefficient -0.0284 (p=0.0465) when continuous growth was also in the model, but the prostate by high-growth interaction was borderline and not significant at 0.05 (interaction -0.0313, p=0.0586).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic single- and pairwise-predicate heterogeneity screen, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_BRAF` is expected to include `braf_v600e == 1` cell lines.
In the same heterogeneity screen, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_EGFR` is expected to include `egfr_amplification == 1` cell lines, with or without high growth-rate modifiers.
In the same heterogeneity screen, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2` is expected to include `erbb2_amplification == 1` cell lines.
In the same heterogeneity screen, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_KRAS` is expected to include `kras_mutation == 1` cell lines.
In the same heterogeneity screen, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_RIT1` is expected to include both `nf1_loss == 1` and `lineage == brain`.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_BRAF subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top BRAF subgroup was `braf_v600e == 1` (n=5574), effect -0.5552 vs the complement.
analyses.search_scope: all dependency outcomes; single and pairwise predicates from lineage, subtype, culture, media, mutation/CNA features, and high/low quartiles of continuous features

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_EGFR subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The most negative EGFR subgroup was `egfr_amplification == 1` and high growth (n=964), effect -0.3526; the single `egfr_amplification == 1` predicate was nearly identical (effect -0.3486).

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_ERBB2 subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top ERBB2 subgroup was `erbb2_amplification == 1` (n=4890), effect -0.4321.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_KRAS subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top KRAS subgroup was `kras_mutation == 1` (n=11842), effect -0.3075.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_RIT1 subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top RIT1 subgroup was `lineage == brain` and `nf1_loss == 1` (n=329), effect -0.1397.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the systematic heterogeneity screen, `lineage == colorectal` and `apc_mutation == 1` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, `lineage == prostate` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` cell lines have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` cell lines have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` cell lines have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, `myc_amplification == 1` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` cell lines have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_KIF18A subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top pairwise KIF18A subgroup was `lineage == colorectal` and `apc_mutation == 1` (n=3925), effect -0.6899; adding high WNT and SMAD4 intact was tested in later refinement.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_TMED10 subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top TMED10 subgroup was `lineage == prostate` and high growth (n=621), effect -0.0533, but later regression showed this modifier was weak after conditioning on growth.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_DHX9 subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The high-growth/high-MYC RNA subgroup (n=3320) had effect -0.0595 for DHX9 dependency; a smaller squamous/high-MYC subgroup had effect -0.0656 but weaker p-value because of sample size.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_POLR2A subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top POLR2A subgroup was high growth (n=12577), effect -0.1043.

analyses.code: Systematic scan over 68 single predicates and all eligible pairs; Welch tests for dependency_RPL11 subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: The top RPL11 subgroup was MYC amplification and high growth (n=2557), effect -0.1303.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_BRAF` is `braf_v600e == 1`, with more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than `braf_v600e == 0`; no tested skin-lineage enhancement is required.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_EGFR` is `egfr_amplification == 1`, with more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than `egfr_amplification == 0`.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2` is `erbb2_amplification == 1`, with more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than `erbb2_amplification == 0`.

The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_KRAS` is `kras_mutation == 1`, with more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than `kras_mutation == 0`; no tested pancreatic-lineage enhancement is required.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_RIT1` is `nf1_loss == 1`, with `lineage == brain` acting as an enhancer that makes the NF1-loss subgroup still more negative.

The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A` is `lineage == colorectal`, `apc_mutation == 1`, high `wnt_activity_score` (`>= 0.819`), and `smad4_loss == 0`, with much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other lines.

The final best-supported pattern for `dependency_TMED10` is a modest broad high-growth effect, with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`>= 0.964`) lines more negative than lower-growth lines; no strong lineage modifier was confirmed.

The final best-supported pattern for `dependency_DHX9` is a modest MYC/proliferation-linked effect, with high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` lines more negative than other lines but without a significant interaction.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_POLR2A` is high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`>= 0.964`), with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than lower-growth lines.

The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_RPL11` is `myc_amplification == 1`, with the `myc_amplification == 1` and high-growth subgroup showing the most negative raw subgroup contrast but no significant MYC-by-growth interaction.

analyses.code: Consolidation of adjusted driver-gene main-effect regressions and lineage interaction checks
analyses.result_summary: Matched alteration effects remained the best explanation for BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, and KRAS dependencies: BRAF V600E -0.5545, EGFR amplification -0.3489, ERBB2 amplification -0.4325, KRAS mutation -0.3095. Skin and pancreatic lineage interactions were not significant for BRAF and KRAS respectively.

analyses.code: Adjusted NF1-loss regression plus NF1-loss by brain interaction
analyses.result_summary: NF1 loss had adjusted effect -0.0955 on RIT1 dependency, and the brain-lineage interaction added -0.0579 (p=0.00582).

analyses.code: Complete subgroup test and SMAD4 suppressor interaction for dependency_KIF18A
analyses.result_summary: The complete KIF18A subgroup had effect -1.0460 vs all other lines, while SMAD4 loss suppressed this dependency by about +1.04 units.

analyses.code: Consolidation of growth/MYC dependency models for TMED10, DHX9, POLR2A, and RPL11
analyses.result_summary: High growth was associated with POLR2A (-0.1044) and TMED10 (-0.0370) dependency; MYC RNA was associated with DHX9 (-0.0167 per SD); MYC amplification was associated with RPL11 (-0.1043). Interaction tests for DHX9 high-MYC by high-growth and RPL11 MYC-amplification by high-growth were not significant at 0.05.
